Your task is to transform the existing game outline for "Stress Busters – Superhero Training Academy" into a coding framework suitable for implementation. This framework should allow for an interactive experience, including a Kannada version of the game, ensuring inclusivity across different age groups and backgrounds. 

---

The structure should be modular, incorporating each level and its specific mechanics, objectives, and points systems. Ensure that the coding framework accommodates both English and Kannada languages seamlessly.

---

For example, each level should be encapsulated in functions or classes that define the following:

Level Name: 

Time Limit: 

Game Type: 

Objectives: 

Mechanics: 

Points System: 
---

Be mindful to include elements that promote engagement, such as interactive quizzes, team battles, and creative sharing. Ensure that all components align with the core psychological principles of cognitive appraisal and coping strategies while being relatable to the target age group.

---

Additionally, avoid overly complex coding structures that may hinder gameplay for younger players. The final output should be a clear, organized code outline that can be further developed into a full game.

---

Examples of coding structures may include:

```
def level_one():
    # Code for Level 1: Meet the Stress Monster
    pass

def level_two():
    # Code for Level 2: The Embarrassment Arena
    pass
```

---

Ensure the code is easy to read and well-commented to facilitate understanding for those who may work on it in the future.

ASTER PROMPT FOR AI MODEL

You are an expert educational program designer, child psychologist, curriculum developer, and Python app developer.

Your task is to design a complete one-hour confidence-building module for students aged 5 to 18 years (create age variations within the same framework). The module must include:

A structured session plan

Interactive activities

A gamified Python app concept with working sample code

Printable materials

Reflection tools

A complete Kannada translation of the module

MODULE DETAILS
Module Title:

"Confidence is a Skill: Learn It, Practice It, Grow It"

1️⃣ OVERVIEW OF THE MODULE

This module is designed to help students understand that confidence is not something you are born with — it is a skill that can be learned and strengthened.

The session must include:

Fun games

Guided reflections

Role-plays

Interactive discussions

Group participation

Leadership mini-activities

The tone must be:

Age-appropriate

Encouraging

Interactive

Safe and inclusive

2️⃣ PURPOSE OF THE MODULE

Design the module to help students:

• Identify their strengths and talents
• Manage fear, hesitation, and self-doubt
• Develop communication skills
• Express themselves confidently
• Build resilience
• Improve teamwork and leadership

3️⃣ LEARNING OUTCOMES

By the end of the session, students should demonstrate:

A. Self-Awareness Skills

Identify strengths

Recognize emotions

Understand how thoughts affect actions

B. Confidence Skills

Speak in front of others with reduced hesitation

Try new tasks

Use positive self-talk

Develop a growth mindset

C. Communication & Social Skills

Eye contact

Good posture

Clear voice

Respectful expression of opinions

Teamwork participation

D. Resilience Skills

Accept mistakes

Keep trying

Ask for help when needed

4️⃣ SESSION STRUCTURE (60 MINUTES)

Design detailed facilitator instructions for each section.

Warm-Up (5 minutes)

Include:

Icebreaker game (e.g., Two Truths and a Dream)

Fun movement/stretch activity

Quick confidence poll

Core Learning (25 minutes)

Include:

Role-play scenarios

Group discussion questions

Confidence challenge activities

Mini leadership tasks

Thought reframing activity

Provide:

Exact facilitator script

Student instructions

Materials required

Gamified Activity (15 minutes)

Design a simple Python-based confidence-building game.

The Python app must include:

A simple GUI (Tkinter or Streamlit)

Points system

Levels (Beginner, Brave, Bold)

Confidence challenges

Positive reinforcement messages

Self-reflection scoring

Encouraging feedback

Provide:

Complete working Python code

Commented code explanation

Instructions to run the program

Suggestions to extend the app

Game idea examples:

“Confidence Quest”

“Brave Choice Simulator”

“Self-Talk Transformer”

“Speak Up Challenge”

Make it child-friendly and interactive.

Reflection & Sharing (10 minutes)

Include:

Guided reflection questions

Confidence journal prompts

Group sharing circle structure

Affirmation exercise

Provide printable worksheet format.

Closing (5 minutes)

Include:

Confidence pledge

Group affirmation chant

Take-home mini challenge

5️⃣ DIFFERENTIATE BY AGE GROUP

Provide modifications for:

Ages 5–8

Ages 9–13

Ages 14–18

Adjust:

Language

Role-play difficulty

Reflection depth

Game complexity

6️⃣ MATERIALS SECTION

Provide:

Printable worksheets (text format)

Reflection sheet template

Strengths chart template

Facilitator checklist

Confidence assessment mini scale

7️⃣ KANNADA VERSION

Translate the following fully into Kannada:

Module overview

Objectives

Session structure

Key activities

Reflection prompts

Confidence pledge

Ensure:

Simple and clear Kannada

Child-friendly wording

Culturally appropriate examples

8️⃣ BONUS REQUIREMENTS

Also include:

How to measure impact (simple pre-post confidence scale)

Observation checklist for teachers

Parent involvement suggestions

Weekly follow-up plan

4-week extension curriculum outline

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIRED

Structure the response with clear headings:

Complete Module Plan

Detailed Facilitator Script

Python App Code

Printable Worksheets

Age Modifications

Impact Measurement Plan

Kannada Translation

Make the module highly interactive, practical, and implementable in schools.

Ensure the final output is professional, structured, and ready for immediate use.

```python name=confidence_module.py
"""
==========================================
CONFIDENCE IS A SKILL: LEARN IT, PRACTICE IT, GROW IT
Psychoeducational Module for Ages 5-18
==========================================

Module Creator: Shreya Choudhary
Version: 2.0
Language Support: English & Kannada
Last Updated: March 2026

OVERVIEW:
This module provides a structured, interactive, and gamified approach to building
confidence across different age groups (5-18 years). It includes psychoeducational
techniques, role-based activities, age-differentiated strategies, and a complete
Python gaming framework.

KEY FEATURES:
✓ Age-specific confidence techniques (5-7, 8-10, 11-13, 14-18)
✓ Role-based interactive games (Facilitator, Student, Parent, Teacher)
✓ Psychoeducational principles with cognitive reframing
✓ Gamification with points, levels, and achievements
✓ Complete Kannada translation
✓ Printable worksheets & reflection tools
✓ Interactive facilitator scripts
"""

import random
import json
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum


# =========== ENUMS & CONSTANTS ===========

class AgeGroup(Enum):
    """Age group classification"""
    YOUNG_CHILD = "5-7"
    CHILD = "8-10"
    PRETEEN = "11-13"
    TEENAGER = "14-18"


class ConfidenceLevel(Enum):
    """Confidence achievement levels"""
    BEGINNER = 1
    BRAVE = 2
    BOLD = 3
    SUPERHERO = 4


class Activity(Enum):
    """Activity types"""
    REFLECTION = "reflection"
    ROLE_PLAY = "role_play"
    GAME = "game"
    DISCUSSION = "discussion"
    PHYSICAL = "physical"
    CREATIVE = "creative"


# =========== DATA CLASSES ===========

@dataclass
class ConfidenceChallenge:
    """Individual confidence challenge structure"""
    challenge_id: str
    age_group: AgeGroup
    title: str
    description: str
    activity_type: Activity
    difficulty: int
    points: int
    techniques: List[str]
    scripts: Dict[str, str]
    reflection_questions: List[str]


@dataclass
class StudentProgress:
    """Track student progress through modules"""
    student_id: str
    age_group: AgeGroup
    current_level: ConfidenceLevel
    total_points: int
    challenges_completed: List[str]
    strengths_identified: List[str]
    confidence_score: float  # 0-100


# =========== CONFIDENCE BUILDING FRAMEWORK ===========

class ConfidenceModule:
    """
    Main module class managing all confidence-building activities
    and progression through levels.
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize the confidence module"""
        self.students = {}
        self.challenges = self._initialize_challenges()
        self.language = "english"  # Default language
        self.translations = self._load_translations()
    
    def _initialize_challenges(self) -> Dict[str, ConfidenceChallenge]:
        """Initialize all confidence challenges across age groups"""
        challenges = {}
        
        # ===== AGES 5-7: "THE CONFIDENT EXPLORER" =====
        challenges['c501'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c501',
            age_group=AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD,
            title="Mirror, Mirror - Express Yourself!",
            description="Children learn to recognize emotions and express themselves through mirroring",
            activity_type=Activity.PHYSICAL,
            difficulty=1,
            points=100,
            techniques=[
                "Emotional recognition",
                "Non-verbal expression",
                "Self-awareness"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - Mirror, Mirror (Ages 5-7)
                Duration: 8 minutes
                
                STEP 1: WARM-UP (2 min)
                Say: "Today we're going to play a fun game where you become mirrors!
                A mirror shows exactly what it sees. You'll show exactly what I show!"
                
                STEP 2: EMOTION MIRRORING (4 min)
                - Show happy face → children mirror
                - Show sad face → children mirror
                - Show proud face (chest out, smile big) → children mirror
                - Show brave face (determined look) → children mirror
                
                KEY PHRASES:
                "When you feel brave, you can stand tall like this!"
                "Happy feelings make us smile big!"
                
                STEP 3: MOVEMENT MIRRORING (2 min)
                Do confident movements:
                - Walk with head held high
                - Give yourself a thumbs up
                - Take a deep proud breath
                - Strike a superhero pose
                
                CLOSING: "You're all mirrors of confidence!"
                """,
                "parent": """
                PARENT GUIDANCE:
                - Play this at home during meals
                - Focus on POSITIVE emotions
                - Praise: "I love how confidently you showed that!"
                - Use phrases like "You're so brave!" and "I'm so proud!"
                """,
                "student": """
                KID'S INSTRUCTION:
                Watch your teacher/parent and copy everything!
                Show big happy smiles, proud faces, and brave movements!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "Which happy face did you like best?",
                "How does your body feel when you're brave?",
                "Can you show me your bravest face again?"
            ]
        )
        
        challenges['c502'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c502',
            age_group=AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD,
            title="My Superpower Colors",
            description="Children identify their strengths using colors and creative expression",
            activity_type=Activity.CREATIVE,
            difficulty=1,
            points=120,
            techniques=[
                "Strength identification",
                "Positive self-concept",
                "Creative expression"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - My Superpower Colors (Ages 5-7)
                Duration: 10 minutes
                
                MATERIALS NEEDED:
                - Colored papers
                - Crayons/markers
                - Images of superheroes
                
                STEP 1: SUPERHERO TALK (2 min)
                "Every superhero has special powers! You have special powers too!
                Maybe you're good at drawing, helping friends, singing, or being kind!"
                
                STEP 2: IDENTIFY SUPERPOWERS (3 min)
                Ask questions:
                "Who is good at making friends?" (raise hand)
                "Who is kind to others?" (raise hand)
                "Who is great at trying new things?" (raise hand)
                
                STEP 3: COLOR YOUR SUPERPOWER (4 min)
                "Pick a color for each superpower:
                - Red = BRAVE (you try new things)
                - Blue = KIND (you help others)
                - Yellow = SMART (you learn quickly)
                - Green = CREATIVE (you make cool things)
                - Purple = LEADER (you help guide the team)"
                
                STEP 4: CELEBRATION (1 min)
                "Show me your superpower color! Give yourself a round of applause!"
                """,
                "parent": """
                AT HOME:
                - Notice your child's strengths daily
                - Point them out: "You were so kind when..."
                - Create a "Strength Wall" in your home
                - Use color stickers to track strengths
                """,
                "student": """
                WHAT YOU DO:
                1. Listen to what your superpower might be
                2. Choose your favorite color
                3. Make it colorful and beautiful
                4. Tell everyone your superpower!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "What color is your biggest superpower?",
                "Who helped you discover this superpower?",
                "How can you use your superpower today?"
            ]
        )
        
        # ===== AGES 8-10: "THE BRAVE ADVENTURER" =====
        challenges['c801'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c801',
            age_group=AgeGroup.CHILD,
            title="Fear Detective - Name Your Fears",
            description="Children identify, name, and develop strategies to manage specific fears",
            activity_type=Activity.DISCUSSION,
            difficulty=2,
            points=150,
            techniques=[
                "Fear identification",
                "Cognitive reframing",
                "Coping strategy development"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - Fear Detective (Ages 8-10)
                Duration: 12 minutes
                
                INTRODUCTION (2 min):
                "Everyone feels scared sometimes. Even superheroes! The secret is
                understanding our fears and making a plan to be brave anyway.
                Today we're Fear Detectives!"
                
                STEP 1: FEAR BRAINSTORM (3 min)
                "What are some things that make kids feel scared?"
                Write on board: Speaking in class, making mistakes, trying new things,
                being left out, failing, looking silly
                
                STEP 2: FEAR INVESTIGATION (4 min)
                Activity: "Fear in a Hat"
                - Write common fears on papers
                - Draw one fear randomly
                - Discuss: "Is this fear real right now?" (Usually NO!)
                - This shows fears are often about "what if" not "what is"
                
                KEY INSIGHT:
                "Most of our fears never actually happen. Our brain is trying to
                protect us, but it can imagine scary things that probably won't occur."
                
                STEP 3: BRAVE RESPONSE PLAN (3 min)
                For each fear:
                - PAUSE and breathe (show deep breathing)
                - CHECK: Is this real RIGHT NOW? (usually no)
                - PLAN: What can I actually do?
                - ACT: Take one small brave step
                
                EXAMPLE:
                Fear: "I'll mess up my presentation"
                Check: "Am I messing up right now?" (No, I haven't started)
                Plan: "I can practice, I can ask for help"
                Act: "I'll practice once tonight"
                """,
                "parent": """
                SUPPORTING YOUR CHILD AT HOME:
                
                DO:
                - Listen to fears without dismissing them
                - Validate: "That feeling makes sense"
                - Help them reality-check: "What's actually happening?"
                - Break big fears into small manageable steps
                - Celebrate brave attempts (not just successes)
                
                DON'T:
                - Say "Don't be scared" (dismissive)
                - Force them into scary situations
                - Share your own anxieties
                - Compare to other kids
                
                CONVERSATION STARTER:
                "What's something you felt scared about today? What did you discover?"
                """,
                "student": """
                YOUR FEAR DETECTIVE MISSION:
                
                1. NOTICE: What makes you feel scared?
                2. NAME IT: Give your fear a name (ex: "The Test Monster")
                3. CHECK: Is it really happening right now?
                4. PLAN: What's ONE small thing you can do?
                5. ACT: Do it!
                6. CELEBRATE: You were brave!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "What fear did you discover today?",
                "What did you learn about this fear?",
                "What's one brave thing you can do this week?",
                "How does it feel to understand your fear better?"
            ]
        )
        
        challenges['c802'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c802',
            age_group=AgeGroup.CHILD,
            title="Mistake Master - Turning Failures into Wins",
            description="Children reframe mistakes as learning opportunities and build resilience",
            activity_type=Activity.ROLE_PLAY,
            difficulty=2,
            points=150,
            techniques=[
                "Growth mindset",
                "Resilience building",
                "Error normalization"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - Mistake Master (Ages 8-10)
                Duration: 12 minutes
                
                HOOK (1 min):
                "I'm going to tell you a secret: The most successful people in the
                world make LOTS of mistakes. They just don't give up!"
                
                STEP 1: MISTAKE CELEBRATION (3 min)
                Show examples:
                - Thomas Edison tried 1000 times before light bulb worked
                - Michael Jordan missed thousands of shots
                - J.K. Rowling was rejected many times
                
                Activity: "Mistake Trophy"
                - Everyone shares ONE recent mistake
                - Instead of shame → celebrate it!
                - "That's a GOLD MEDAL mistake! You learned something!"
                
                STEP 2: THE MAGIC PHRASE (3 min)
                Teach: "I'm not good at this YET."
                
                Instead of: "I can't do it"
                Say: "I can't do it YET"
                
                Practice phrases:
                "I made a mistake AND I'm learning"
                "This is hard AND I can try again"
                "I failed AND that's how I learn"
                
                STEP 3: ROLE-PLAY SCENARIOS (4 min)
                Scenario 1: Mess up homework
                - Poor response: Give up, feel bad
                - Brave response: Notice error, fix it, ask for help
                
                Scenario 2: Forget lines in school play
                - Poor response: Feel embarrassed, quit
                - Brave response: Take breath, keep going, laugh it off
                
                Scenario 3: Lose a game
                - Poor response: Blame others, feel defeated
                - Brave response: Review what happened, practice more
                
                CLOSING:
                "Mistakes are proof you're trying. Thank them!"
                """,
                "parent": """
                BUILDING RESILIENCE AT HOME:
                
                WHEN YOUR CHILD MAKES A MISTAKE:
                1. Stay calm (your reaction sets the tone)
                2. Validate: "That's frustrating"
                3. Normalize: "Everyone makes mistakes"
                4. Explore: "What can we learn?"
                5. Plan: "What will you try differently?"
                6. Encourage: "I'm proud you tried"
                
                LANGUAGE MATTERS:
                Instead of: "You're so smart" (fixed)
                Say: "You worked really hard" (growth)
                
                Instead of: "That's bad"
                Say: "That didn't work. What's next?"
                """,
                "student": """
                BECOME A MISTAKE MASTER:
                
                When you make a mistake:
                □ Take a deep breath
                □ Say "I'm learning"
                □ Ask "What can I do differently?"
                □ Try again
                □ Celebrate yourself!
                
                Remember: Mistakes = Learning Opportunities!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "What's a mistake you made recently?",
                "What did you learn from it?",
                "How does it feel to think of mistakes as learning?",
                "Who is someone who taught you by making mistakes?"
            ]
        )
        
        # ===== AGES 11-13: "THE CONFIDENT LEADER" =====
        challenges['c1101'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c1101',
            age_group=AgeGroup.PRETEEN,
            title="Voice & Vision - Speak Your Truth",
            description="Pre-teens develop assertive communication and learn to express opinions respectfully",
            activity_type=Activity.ROLE_PLAY,
            difficulty=3,
            points=200,
            techniques=[
                "Assertive communication",
                "Active listening",
                "Boundary setting",
                "Respectful disagreement"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - Voice & Vision (Ages 11-13)
                Duration: 15 minutes
                
                CONTEXT:
                This age group wants to be heard but often struggles with:
                - Fear of judgment from peers
                - Worry about being "different"
                - Difficulty balancing respect with assertiveness
                
                OPENING (2 min):
                "You have opinions. You have ideas. You have a VOICE.
                But sometimes it feels scary to use it. Today we learn HOW."
                
                STEP 1: VOICE TYPES EDUCATION (3 min)
                
                SILENT VOICE: Not speaking (stays quiet, agrees with everything)
                Problem: Others don't know your real thoughts
                
                AGGRESSIVE VOICE: Speaking without respect (yelling, rude)
                Problem: People don't listen, relationships hurt
                
                ASSERTIVE VOICE: Speaking with confidence AND respect
                Solution: Clear, kind, honest - THIS is the goal!
                
                STEP 2: ASSERTIVE LANGUAGE PRACTICE (5 min)
                
                Situation 1: Friend always copies your ideas
                Aggressive: "Stop copying me! You're so annoying!"
                Passive: *stays silent and feels frustrated*
                Assertive: "I notice you often use my ideas. I'd prefer you develop 
                           your own ideas. Can we work on that?"
                
                Situation 2: Parent/Teacher unfair decision
                Aggressive: "That's SO unfair! You're not listening!"
                Passive: Say nothing, feel resentful
                Assertive: "I understand your decision. I'd like to explain my 
                          perspective. Can you listen?"
                
                Situation 3: Peer pressure (wrong activity)
                Aggressive: "That's dumb! You're all idiots!"
                Passive: Go along even though uncomfortable
                Assertive: "I understand why you want to do that. It's not for me. 
                          I'll do [this] instead."
                
                KEY FORMULA FOR ASSERTIVE VOICE:
                1. USE "I" (I think, I feel, I believe)
                2. BE SPECIFIC (not "that's mean" but "when you...")
                3. SHOW RESPECT (acknowledge other person's view)
                4. STATE YOUR NEED (I need you to...)
                5. PROPOSE SOLUTION (how about we...)
                
                STEP 3: LIVE ROLE-PLAY (4 min)
                Pair work:
                - Student 1: Plays scenario
                - Student 2: Responds assertively
                - Facilitator coaches
                - Swap roles
                
                STEP 4: CHALLENGE LADDER (1 min)
                Easy: Speak in small group
                Medium: Raise hand in class
                Hard: Share unpopular opinion
                Expert: Lead discussion on topic you care about
                
                CLOSING:
                "Your voice matters. Use it with respect AND confidence."
                """,
                "parent": """
                SUPPORTING ASSERTIVE COMMUNICATION AT HOME:
                
                ENCOURAGE:
                - Ask their opinions on family decisions
                - Listen fully without interrupting
                - Validate views even if you disagree
                - Model respectful disagreement
                
                COACH:
                - Help them find their words
                - Practice difficult conversations
                - Teach them "I" statements
                - Show how to stay calm under pressure
                
                AVOID:
                - "Don't talk back" (kills authentic expression)
                - Making them feel wrong for having opinions
                - Speaking for them ("He's shy")
                - Dismissing their ideas as "not important"
                
                CONVERSATION:
                "What do you want to say but feel nervous about?
                Let's practice together."
                """,
                "student": """
                FINDING YOUR ASSERTIVE VOICE:
                
                Remember the 5 Steps:
                1️⃣ USE "I" - It's about you, your feelings
                2️⃣ BE SPECIFIC - Give examples
                3️⃣ SHOW RESPECT - They deserve it
                4️⃣ STATE YOUR NEED - Be clear what you want
                5️⃣ PROPOSE SOLUTION - Work together
                
                Practice Sentences:
                • "I think... because..."
                • "I feel... when you..."
                • "I need... from you..."
                • "How about we..."
                • "I appreciate your view AND I believe..."
                
                CONFIDENCE BUILDER:
                Speak up once a day in a safe place!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "When do you find it hardest to speak your truth?",
                "What would happen if you shared your real opinion?",
                "Who models good assertive communication for you?",
                "What's one thing you want to say this week?",
                "How does your body feel when you speak assertively?"
            ]

# =========== GAMIFICATION ENGINE ===========

class ConfidenceGame:
    """
    Interactive gamified experience with points, levels, and achievements
    """
    
    def __init__(self, student_id: str, age_group: AgeGroup):
        """Initialize game instance"""
        self.student_id = student_id
        self.age_group = age_group
        self.current_level = ConfidenceLevel.BEGINNER
        self.points = 0
        self.achievements = []
        self.completed_challenges = []
        self.confidence_score = 50.0  # 0-100 scale
        self.start_time = datetime.now()
    
    def complete_challenge(self, challenge: ConfidenceChallenge, 
                          performance_score: float) -> Dict:
        """
        Complete a challenge and calculate rewards
        
        Args:
            challenge: The challenge completed
            performance_score: Score out of 100
        
        Returns:
            Dictionary with points, level up info, etc.
        """
        # Calculate points (base points + performance bonus)
        base_points = challenge.points
        bonus = int(base_points * (performance_score / 100) * 0.5)
        total_points = base_points + bonus
        
        self.points += total_points
        self.completed_challenges.append(challenge.challenge_id)
        
        # Update confidence score
        self.confidence_score = min(100, self.confidence_score + (performance_score * 0.2))
        
        # Check for level up
        level_up = self._check_level_up()
        
        # Generate achievement
        achievement = self._generate_achievement(challenge, performance_score)
        if achievement:
            self.achievements.append(achievement)
        
        return {
            "points_earned": total_points,
            "total_points": self.points,
            "confidence_score": self.confidence_score,
            "level_up": level_up,
            "achievement": achievement,
            "message": self._generate_feedback(performance_score)
        }
    
    def _check_level_up(self) -> bool:
        """Check if player levels up based on points"""
        thresholds = {
            ConfidenceLevel.BRAVE: 500,
            ConfidenceLevel.BOLD: 1500,
            ConfidenceLevel.SUPERHERO: 3000
        }
        
        for level, threshold in thresholds.items():
            if self.points >= threshold and self.current_level.value < level.value:
                self.current_level = level
                return True
        return False
    
    def _generate_achievement(self, challenge: ConfidenceChallenge, 
                             score: float) -> Dict or None:
        """Generate achievement badge if earned"""
        if score >= 90:
            return {
                "name": "Perfect Confidence!",
                "description": f"Mastered {challenge.title}",
                "icon": "⭐"
            }
        elif score >= 75 and challenge.challenge_id not in self.completed_challenges:
            return {
                "name": "Challenge Conquered!",
                "description": f"Completed {challenge.title}",
                "icon": "🏆"
            }
        return None
    
    def _generate_feedback(self, score: float) -> str:
        """Generate personalized feedback"""
        if score >= 90:
            responses = [
                "🌟 Absolutely incredible! You're a confidence superhero!",
                "🚀 Outstanding performance! You're crushing it!",
                "💫 Perfect! You nailed this challenge!",
            ]
        elif score >= 75:
            responses = [
                "✨ Great work! You're building real confidence!",
                "🎉 Excellent! You handled that beautifully!",
                "👏 Well done! You're making progress!",
            ]
        elif score >= 50:
            responses = [
                "💪 Good effort! You're learning and growing!",
                "🌱 Nice try! Keep practicing these skills!",
                "🎯 You've got this! Keep going!",
            ]
        else:
            responses = [
                "🌟 Every attempt teaches you something! Keep trying!",
                "💡 Learning is messy - and that's perfect!",
                "🚀 You showed up - that's what matters!",
            ]
        
        return random.choice(responses)
    
    def get_progress_report(self) -> Dict:
        """Generate detailed progress report"""
        return {
            "student_id": self.student_id,
            "age_group": self.age_group.value,
            "current_level": self.current_level.name,
            "total_points": self.points,
            "confidence_score": self.confidence_score,
            "challenges_completed": len(self.completed_challenges),
            "achievements": len(self.achievements),
            "session_duration": str(datetime.now() - self.start_time),
            "achievements_list": self.achievements,
            "next_milestone": f"{self.current_level.value + 1}" if self.current_level.value < 4 else "SUPERHERO!"
        }

class ReflectionActivity(InteractiveActivity):
    """Guided reflection prompts based on age and challenge"""
    
    def run_activity(self, questions: List[str]) -> Dict:
        """
        Run reflection activity with guided questions
        
        Args:
            questions: List of reflection questions
        
        Returns:
            Dictionary with responses and insights
        """
        responses = []
        for i, question in enumerate(questions, 1):
            print(f"\n📝 Question {i}: {question}")
            response = input("Your response: ").strip()
            responses.append({
                "question": question,
                "response": response,
                "depth_score": self._score_response_depth(response)
            })
        
        return {
            "activity_type": "reflection",
            "responses": responses,
            "avg_depth": sum(r["depth_score"] for r in responses) / len(responses),
            "insights": self._generate_insights(responses)
        }
    
    def _score_response_depth(self, response: str) -> float:
        """Score how deeply someone reflected (0-100)"""
        # Simple heuristic: longer, more detailed responses score higher
        words = len(response.split())
        depth_score = min(100, words * 5)
        
        # Bonus for emotional/self-aware language
        reflective_words = [
            "feel", "think", "realize", "understand", "discover",
            "learn", "because", "when", "since", "although"
        ]
        if any(word in response.lower() for word in reflective_words):
            depth_score += 15
        
        return depth_score
    
    def _generate_insights(self, responses: List[Dict]) -> str:
        """Generate personalized insights from responses"""
        avg_depth = sum(r["depth_score"] for r in responses) / len(responses)
        
        if avg_depth > 75:
            return "Wow! You're doing some really deep reflection. That's how real growth happens!"
        elif avg_depth > 50:
            return "Good reflection! Keep going deeper by asking 'why' and 'what does this mean to me?'"
        else:
            return "Reflection takes practice. Try being more specific about feelings and reasons."


class RolePlayActivity(InteractiveActivity):
    """Interactive role-play scenarios"""
    
    def __init__(self, age_group: AgeGroup):
        super().__init__(Activity.ROLE_PLAY, age_group)
        self.scenarios = self._get_scenarios_by_age()
    
    def _get_scenarios_by_age(self) -> List[Dict]:
        """Get age-appropriate role-play scenarios"""
        scenarios_by_age = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD: [
                {
                    "title": "Making a New Friend",
                    "context": "You see someone playing alone at recess",
                    "steps": ["Approach kindly", "Say hello", "Suggest playing together"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Asking for Help",
                    "context": "You don't understand math homework",
                    "steps": ["Raise hand", "Say 'I need help'", "Explain what's confusing"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.CHILD: [
                {
                    "title": "Speaking Up About Feelings",
                    "context": "A friend took your idea without asking",
                    "steps": ["Choose calm time", "Describe what happened", "Explain how it felt", "Suggest solution"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Handling a Mistake",
                    "context": "You got something wrong in class",
                    "steps": ["Take breath", "Own it", "Learn from it", "Try again"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.PRETEEN: [
                {
                    "title": "Respectful Disagreement",
                    "context": "You disagree with a parent's decision",
                    "steps": ["State your view calmly", "Listen to their perspective", "Propose compromise", "Respect final decision"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Peer Pressure Resistance",
                    "context": "Friends want to do something you're uncomfortable with",
                    "steps": ["Acknowledge their idea", "State your boundary", "Offer alternative", "Don't justify endlessly"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.TEENAGER: [
                {
                    "title": "Authentic Self Expression",
                    "context": "You want to express an unpopular opinion",
                    "steps": ["Know your values", "Choose your audience", "Speak with confidence", "Handle disagreement"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Setting Healthy Boundaries",
                    "context": "Someone's behavior is affecting your wellbeing",
                    "steps": ["Identify the issue", "Choose the person", "Express clearly", "Enforce boundary"]
                }
            ]
        }
        
        return scenarios_by_age.get(self.age_group, [])
    
    def run_scenario(self, scenario: Dict) -> Dict:
        """Run a role-play scenario"""
        print(f"\n🎭 ROLE-PLAY SCENARIO: {scenario['title']}")
        print(f"Context: {scenario['context']}")
        print("\nSteps to practice:")
        for i, step in enumerate(scenario['steps'], 1):
            print(f"  {i}. {step}")
        
        responses = []
        for i, step in enumerate(scenario['steps'], 1):
            print(f"\n📢 Now try Step {i}: {step}")
            response = input("What would you say/do? ")
            responses.append({
                "step": step,
                "response": response,
                "effectiveness": self._score_response(step, response)
            })
        
        return {
            "scenario": scenario['title'],
            "responses": responses,
            "overall_score": sum(r["effectiveness"] for r in responses) / len(responses),
            "feedback": self._generate_role_play_feedback(responses)
        }
    
    def _score_response(self, step: str, response: str) -> float:
        """Score quality of role-play response"""
        # Heuristics for quality
        score = 50.0
        
        # Length indicates effort
        if len(response) > 20:
            score += 15
        
        # Respectful/calm language
        calm_indicators = ["please", "thank you", "i understand", "i appreciate"]
        if any(indicator in response.lower() for indicator in calm_indicators):
            score += 15
        
        # Specific and actionable
        if any(word in response.lower() for word in ["because", "can", "will", "let's"]):
            score += 10
        
        return min(100, score)
    
    def _generate_role_play_feedback(self, responses: List[Dict]) -> str:
        """Generate feedback on role-play"""
        avg_score = sum(r["effectiveness"] for r in responses) / len(responses)
        
        if avg_score >= 80:
            return "🌟 Excellent! Your responses showed real maturity and respect!"
        elif avg_score >= 60:
            return "👍 Good effort! A few tweaks and you'll be even more effective."
        else:
            return "🌱 You're learning! Try being more specific and respectful next time."

ASTER PROMPT FOR AI MODEL

You are an expert educational program designer, child psychologist, curriculum developer, and Python app developer.

Your task is to design a complete one-hour confidence-building module for students aged 5 to 18 years (create age variations within the same framework). The module must include:

A structured session plan

Interactive activities

A gamified Python app concept with working sample code

Printable materials

Reflection tools

A complete Kannada translation of the module

MODULE DETAILS
Module Title:

"Confidence is a Skill: Learn It, Practice It, Grow It"

1️⃣ OVERVIEW OF THE MODULE

This module is designed to help students understand that confidence is not something you are born with — it is a skill that can be learned and strengthened.

The session must include:

Fun games

Guided reflections

Role-plays

Interactive discussions

Group participation

Leadership mini-activities

The tone must be:

Age-appropriate

Encouraging

Interactive

Safe and inclusive

2️⃣ PURPOSE OF THE MODULE

Design the module to help students:

• Identify their strengths and talents
• Manage fear, hesitation, and self-doubt
• Develop communication skills
• Express themselves confidently
• Build resilience
• Improve teamwork and leadership

3️⃣ LEARNING OUTCOMES

By the end of the session, students should demonstrate:

A. Self-Awareness Skills

Identify strengths

Recognize emotions

Understand how thoughts affect actions

B. Confidence Skills

Speak in front of others with reduced hesitation

Try new tasks

Use positive self-talk

Develop a growth mindset

C. Communication & Social Skills

Eye contact

Good posture

Clear voice

Respectful expression of opinions

Teamwork participation

D. Resilience Skills

Accept mistakes

Keep trying

Ask for help when needed

4️⃣ SESSION STRUCTURE (60 MINUTES)

Design detailed facilitator instructions for each section.

Warm-Up (5 minutes)

Include:

Icebreaker game (e.g., Two Truths and a Dream)

Fun movement/stretch activity

Quick confidence poll

Core Learning (25 minutes)

Include:

Role-play scenarios

Group discussion questions

Confidence challenge activities

Mini leadership tasks

Thought reframing activity

Provide:

Exact facilitator script

Student instructions

Materials required

Gamified Activity (15 minutes)

Design a simple Python-based confidence-building game.

The Python app must include:

A simple GUI (Tkinter or Streamlit)

Points system

Levels (Beginner, Brave, Bold)

Confidence challenges

Positive reinforcement messages

Self-reflection scoring

Encouraging feedback

Provide:

Complete working Python code

Commented code explanation

Instructions to run the program

Suggestions to extend the app

Game idea examples:

“Confidence Quest”

“Brave Choice Simulator”

“Self-Talk Transformer”

“Speak Up Challenge”

Make it child-friendly and interactive.

Reflection & Sharing (10 minutes)

Include:

Guided reflection questions

Confidence journal prompts

Group sharing circle structure

Affirmation exercise

Provide printable worksheet format.

Closing (5 minutes)

Include:

Confidence pledge

Group affirmation chant

Take-home mini challenge

5️⃣ DIFFERENTIATE BY AGE GROUP

Provide modifications for:

Ages 5–8

Ages 9–13

Ages 14–18

Adjust:

Language

Role-play difficulty

Reflection depth

Game complexity

6️⃣ MATERIALS SECTION

Provide:

Printable worksheets (text format)

Reflection sheet template

Strengths chart template

Facilitator checklist

Confidence assessment mini scale

7️⃣ KANNADA VERSION

Translate the following fully into Kannada:

Module overview

Objectives

Session structure

Key activities

Reflection prompts

Confidence pledge

Ensure:

Simple and clear Kannada

Child-friendly wording

Culturally appropriate examples

8️⃣ BONUS REQUIREMENTS

Also include:

How to measure impact (simple pre-post confidence scale)

Observation checklist for teachers

Parent involvement suggestions

Weekly follow-up plan

4-week extension curriculum outline

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIRED

Structure the response with clear headings:

Complete Module Plan

Detailed Facilitator Script

Python App Code

Printable Worksheets

Age Modifications

Impact Measurement Plan

Kannada Translation

Make the module highly interactive, practical, and implementable in schools.

Ensure the final output is professional, structured, and ready for immediate use.

"""
==========================================
CONFIDENCE IS A SKILL: LEARN IT, PRACTICE IT, GROW IT
Psychoeducational Module for Ages 5-18
==========================================

Module Creator: Shreya Choudhary
Version: 2.0
Language Support: English & Kannada
Last Updated: March 2026

OVERVIEW:
This module provides a structured, interactive, and gamified approach to building
confidence across different age groups (5-18 years). It includes psychoeducational
techniques, role-based activities, age-differentiated strategies, and a complete
Python gaming framework.

KEY FEATURES:
✓ Age-specific confidence techniques (5-7, 8-10, 11-13, 14-18)
✓ Role-based interactive games (Facilitator, Student, Parent, Teacher)
✓ Psychoeducational principles with cognitive reframing
✓ Gamification with points, levels, and achievements
✓ Complete Kannada translation
✓ Printable worksheets & reflection tools
✓ Interactive facilitator scripts
"""

import random
import json
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum


# =========== ENUMS & CONSTANTS ===========

class AgeGroup(Enum):
    """Age group classification"""
    YOUNG_CHILD = "5-7"
    CHILD = "8-10"
    PRETEEN = "11-13"
    TEENAGER = "14-18"


class ConfidenceLevel(Enum):
    """Confidence achievement levels"""
    BEGINNER = 1
    BRAVE = 2
    BOLD = 3
    SUPERHERO = 4


class Activity(Enum):
    """Activity types"""
    REFLECTION = "reflection"
    ROLE_PLAY = "role_play"
    GAME = "game"
    DISCUSSION = "discussion"
    PHYSICAL = "physical"
    CREATIVE = "creative"


# =========== DATA CLASSES ===========

@dataclass
class ConfidenceChallenge:
    """Individual confidence challenge structure"""
    challenge_id: str
    age_group: AgeGroup
    title: str
    description: str
    activity_type: Activity
    difficulty: int
    points: int
    techniques: List[str]
    scripts: Dict[str, str]
    reflection_questions: List[str]


@dataclass
class StudentProgress:
    """Track student progress through modules"""
    student_id: str
    age_group: AgeGroup
    current_level: ConfidenceLevel
    total_points: int
    challenges_completed: List[str]
    strengths_identified: List[str]
    confidence_score: float  # 0-100


# =========== CONFIDENCE BUILDING FRAMEWORK ===========

class ConfidenceModule:
    """
    Main module class managing all confidence-building activities
    and progression through levels.
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize the confidence module"""
        self.students = {}
        self.challenges = self._initialize_challenges()
        self.language = "english"  # Default language
        self.translations = self._load_translations()
    
    def _initialize_challenges(self) -> Dict[str, ConfidenceChallenge]:
        """Initialize all confidence challenges across age groups"""
        challenges = {}
        
        # ===== AGES 5-7: "THE CONFIDENT EXPLORER" =====
        challenges['c501'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c501',
            age_group=AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD,
            title="Mirror, Mirror - Express Yourself!",
            description="Children learn to recognize emotions and express themselves through mirroring",
            activity_type=Activity.PHYSICAL,
            difficulty=1,
            points=100,
            techniques=[
                "Emotional recognition",
                "Non-verbal expression",
                "Self-awareness"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - Mirror, Mirror (Ages 5-7)
                Duration: 8 minutes
                
                STEP 1: WARM-UP (2 min)
                Say: "Today we're going to play a fun game where you become mirrors!
                A mirror shows exactly what it sees. You'll show exactly what I show!"
                
                STEP 2: EMOTION MIRRORING (4 min)
                - Show happy face → children mirror
                - Show sad face → children mirror
                - Show proud face (chest out, smile big) → children mirror
                - Show brave face (determined look) → children mirror
                
                KEY PHRASES:
                "When you feel brave, you can stand tall like this!"
                "Happy feelings make us smile big!"
                
                STEP 3: MOVEMENT MIRRORING (2 min)
                Do confident movements:
                - Walk with head held high
                - Give yourself a thumbs up
                - Take a deep proud breath
                - Strike a superhero pose
                
                CLOSING: "You're all mirrors of confidence!"
                """,
                "parent": """
                PARENT GUIDANCE:
                - Play this at home during meals
                - Focus on POSITIVE emotions
                - Praise: "I love how confidently you showed that!"
                - Use phrases like "You're so brave!" and "I'm so proud!"
                """,
                "student": """
                KID'S INSTRUCTION:
                Watch your teacher/parent and copy everything!
                Show big happy smiles, proud faces, and brave movements!
                """
            },
            reflection_questions=[
                "Which happy face did you like best?",
                "How does your body feel when you're brave?",
                "Can you show me your bravest face again?"
            ]
        )
        
        challenges['c502'] = ConfidenceChallenge(
            challenge_id='c502',
            age_group=AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD,
            title="My Superpower Colors",
            description="Children identify their strengths using colors and creative expression",
            activity_type=Activity.CREATIVE,
            difficulty=1,
            points=120,
            techniques=[
                "Strength identification",
                "Positive self-concept",
                "Creative expression"
            ],
            scripts={
                "facilitator": """
                FACILITATOR SCRIPT - My Superpower Colors (Ages 5-7)
                Duration: 10 minutes
                
                MATERIALS NEEDED:
                - Colored papers
                - Crayons/markers
                - Images of superheroes
                
                STEP 1: SUPERHERO TALK (2 min)
                "Every superhero has special powers! You have special powers too!
                Maybe you're good at drawing, helping friends, singing, or being kind!"
                
                STEP 2: IDENTIFY SUPERPOWERS (3 min)
                Ask questions:
                "Who is good at making friends?" (raise hand)
                "Who is kind to others?" (raise hand)
                "Who is great at trying new things?" (raise hand)
                
                STEP 3: COLOR YOUR SUPERPOWER (4 min)
                "Pick a color for each superpower:
                - Red = BRAVE (you try new things)
                - Blue = KIND (you help others)
                - Yellow = SMART (you learn quickly)
                - Green = CREATIVE (you make cool things)
                - Purple = LEADER (you help guide the team)"

# =========== GAMIFICATION ENGINE ===========

class ConfidenceGame:
    """
    Interactive gamified experience with points, levels, and achievements
    """
    
    def __init__(self, student_id: str, age_group: AgeGroup):
        """Initialize game instance"""
        self.student_id = student_id
        self.age_group = age_group
        self.current_level = ConfidenceLevel.BEGINNER
        self.points = 0
        self.achievements = []
        self.completed_challenges = []
        self.confidence_score = 50.0  # 0-100 scale
        self.start_time = datetime.now()
    
    def complete_challenge(self, challenge: ConfidenceChallenge, 
                          performance_score: float) -> Dict:
        """
        Complete a challenge and calculate rewards
        
        Args:
            challenge: The challenge completed
            performance_score: Score out of 100
        
        Returns:
            Dictionary with points, level up info, etc.
        """
        # Calculate points (base points + performance bonus)
        base_points = challenge.points
        bonus = int(base_points * (performance_score / 100) * 0.5)
        total_points = base_points + bonus
        
        self.points += total_points
        self.completed_challenges.append(challenge.challenge_id)
        
        # Update confidence score
        self.confidence_score = min(100, self.confidence_score + (performance_score * 0.2))
        
        # Check for level up
        level_up = self._check_level_up()
        
        # Generate achievement
        achievement = self._generate_achievement(challenge, performance_score)
        if achievement:
            self.achievements.append(achievement)
        
        return {
            "points_earned": total_points,
            "total_points": self.points,
            "confidence_score": self.confidence_score,
            "level_up": level_up,
            "achievement": achievement,
            "message": self._generate_feedback(performance_score)
        }
    
    def _check_level_up(self) -> bool:
        """Check if player levels up based on points"""
        thresholds = {
            ConfidenceLevel.BRAVE: 500,
            ConfidenceLevel.BOLD: 1500,
            ConfidenceLevel.SUPERHERO: 3000
        }
        
        for level, threshold in thresholds.items():
            if self.points >= threshold and self.current_level.value < level.value:
                self.current_level = level
                return True
        return False
    
    def _generate_achievement(self, challenge: ConfidenceChallenge, 
                             score: float) -> Dict or None:
        """Generate achievement badge if earned"""
        if score >= 90:
            return {
                "name": "Perfect Confidence!",
                "description": f"Mastered {challenge.title}",
                "icon": "⭐"
            }
        elif score >= 75 and challenge.challenge_id not in self.completed_challenges:
            return {
                "name": "Challenge Conquered!",
                "description": f"Completed {challenge.title}",
                "icon": "🏆"
            }
        return None
    
    def _generate_feedback(self, score: float) -> str:
        """Generate personalized feedback"""
        if score >= 90:
            responses = [
                "🌟 Absolutely incredible! You're a confidence superhero!",
                "🚀 Outstanding performance! You're crushing it!",
                "💫 Perfect! You nailed this challenge!",
            ]
        elif score >= 75:
            responses = [
                "✨ Great work! You're building real confidence!",
                "🎉 Excellent! You handled that beautifully!",
                "👏 Well done! You're making progress!",
            ]
        elif score >= 50:
            responses = [
                "💪 Good effort! You're learning and growing!",
                "🌱 Nice try! Keep practicing these skills!",
                "🎯 You've got this! Keep going!",
            ]
        else:
            responses = [
                "🌟 Every attempt teaches you something! Keep trying!",
                "💡 Learning is messy - and that's perfect!",
                "🚀 You showed up - that's what matters!",
            ]
        
        return random.choice(responses)
    
    def get_progress_report(self) -> Dict:
        """Generate detailed progress report"""
        return {
            "student_id": self.student_id,
            "age_group": self.age_group.value,
            "current_level": self.current_level.name,
            "total_points": self.points,
            "confidence_score": self.confidence_score,
            "challenges_completed": len(self.completed_challenges),
            "achievements": len(self.achievements),
            "session_duration": str(datetime.now() - self.start_time),
            "achievements_list": self.achievements,
            "next_milestone": f"{self.current_level.value + 1}" if self.current_level.value < 4 else "SUPERHERO!"
        }

class ReflectionActivity(InteractiveActivity):
    """Guided reflection prompts based on age and challenge"""
    
    def run_activity(self, questions: List[str]) -> Dict:
        """
        Run reflection activity with guided questions
        
        Args:
            questions: List of reflection questions
        
        Returns:
            Dictionary with responses and insights
        """
        responses = []
        for i, question in enumerate(questions, 1):
            print(f"\n📝 Question {i}: {question}")
            response = input("Your response: ").strip()
            responses.append({
                "question": question,
                "response": response,
                "depth_score": self._score_response_depth(response)
            })
        
        return {
            "activity_type": "reflection",
            "responses": responses,
            "avg_depth": sum(r["depth_score"] for r in responses) / len(responses),
            "insights": self._generate_insights(responses)
        }
    
    def _score_response_depth(self, response: str) -> float:
        """Score how deeply someone reflected (0-100)"""
        # Simple heuristic: longer, more detailed responses score higher
        words = len(response.split())
        depth_score = min(100, words * 5)
        
        # Bonus for emotional/self-aware language
        reflective_words = [
            "feel", "think", "realize", "understand", "discover",
            "learn", "because", "when", "since", "although"
        ]
        if any(word in response.lower() for word in reflective_words):
            depth_score += 15
        
        return depth_score
    
    def _generate_insights(self, responses: List[Dict]) -> str:
        """Generate personalized insights from responses"""
        avg_depth = sum(r["depth_score"] for r in responses) / len(responses)
        
        if avg_depth > 75:
            return "Wow! You're doing some really deep reflection. That's how real growth happens!"
        elif avg_depth > 50:
            return "Good reflection! Keep going deeper by asking 'why' and 'what does this mean to me?'"
        else:
            return "Reflection takes practice. Try being more specific about feelings and reasons."


class RolePlayActivity(InteractiveActivity):
    """Interactive role-play scenarios"""
    
    def __init__(self, age_group: AgeGroup):
        super().__init__(Activity.ROLE_PLAY, age_group)
        self.scenarios = self._get_scenarios_by_age()
    
    def _get_scenarios_by_age(self) -> List[Dict]:
        """Get age-appropriate role-play scenarios"""
        scenarios_by_age = {
            AgeGroup.YOUNG_CHILD: [
                {
                    "title": "Making a New Friend",
                    "context": "You see someone playing alone at recess",
                    "steps": ["Approach kindly", "Say hello", "Suggest playing together"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Asking for Help",
                    "context": "You don't understand math homework",
                    "steps": ["Raise hand", "Say 'I need help'", "Explain what's confusing"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.CHILD: [
                {
                    "title": "Speaking Up About Feelings",
                    "context": "A friend took your idea without asking",
                    "steps": ["Choose calm time", "Describe what happened", "Explain how it felt", "Suggest solution"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Handling a Mistake",
                    "context": "You got something wrong in class",
                    "steps": ["Take breath", "Own it", "Learn from it", "Try again"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.PRETEEN: [
                {
                    "title": "Respectful Disagreement",
                    "context": "You disagree with a parent's decision",
                    "steps": ["State your view calmly", "Listen to their perspective", "Propose compromise", "Respect final decision"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Peer Pressure Resistance",
                    "context": "Friends want to do something you're uncomfortable with",
                    "steps": ["Acknowledge their idea", "State your boundary", "Offer alternative", "Don't justify endlessly"]
                }
            ],
            AgeGroup.TEENAGER: [
                {
                    "title": "Authentic Self Expression",
                    "context": "You want to express an unpopular opinion",
                    "steps": ["Know your values", "Choose your audience", "Speak with confidence", "Handle disagreement"]
                },
                {
                    "title": "Setting Healthy Boundaries",
                    "context": "Someone's behavior is affecting your wellbeing",
                    "steps": ["Identify the issue", "Choose the person", "Express clearly", "Enforce boundary"]
                }
            ]
        }
        
        return scenarios_by_age.get(self.age_group, [])
    
    def run_scenario(self, scenario: Dict) -> Dict:
        """Run a role-play scenario"""
        print(f"\n🎭 ROLE-PLAY SCENARIO: {scenario['title']}")
        print(f"Context: {scenario['context']}")
        print("\nSteps to practice:")
        for i, step in enumerate(scenario['steps'], 1):
            print(f"  {i}. {step}")
        
        responses = []
        for i, step in enumerate(scenario['steps'], 1):
            print(f"\n📢 Now try Step {i}: {step}")
            response = input("What would you say/do? ")
            responses.append({
                "step": step,
                "response": response,
                "effectiveness": self._score_response(step, response)
            })
        
        return {
            "scenario": scenario['title'],
            "responses": responses,
            "overall_score": sum(r["effectiveness"] for r in responses) / len(responses),
            "feedback": self._generate_role_play_feedback(responses)
        }
    
    def _score_response(self, step: str, response: str) -> float:
        """Score quality of role-play response"""
        # Heuristics for quality
        score = 50.0
        
        # Length indicates effort
        if len(response) > 20:
            score += 15
        
        # Respectful/calm language
        calm_indicators = ["please", "thank you", "i understand", "i appreciate"]
        if any(indicator in response.lower() for indicator in calm_indicators):
            score += 15
        
        # Specific and actionable
        if any(word in response.lower() for word in ["because", "can", "will", "let's"]):
            score += 10
        
        return min(100, score)
    
    def _generate_role_play_feedback(self, responses: List[Dict]) -> str:
        """Generate feedback on role-play"""
        avg_score = sum(r["effectiveness"] for r in responses) / len(responses)
        
        if avg_score >= 80:
            return "🌟 Excellent! Your responses showed real maturity and respect!"
        elif avg_score >= 60:
            return "👍 Good effort! A few tweaks and you'll be even more effective."
        else:
            return "🌱 You're learning! Try being more specific and respectful next time."
